news-global-econ.title: 'Let's brew it in the United States': Teamsters target Modelo and Corona in push for Mexican beer tariffs
news-global-econ.description: The Teamsters are pushing Trump to impose tariffs as high as 75% on Mexican beer imports like Modelo and Corona to protect U.S. brewery jobs.

news-global-econ.description:
AI startup Anthropic is scheduling investor meetings ahead of a possible IPO, advancing preparations for what could be one of the year's most significant technology listings after Elon Musk's blockbuster SpaceX public offering.

news-global-econ.title: Helen Mirren re-lists historic Hollywood Hills estate after $6.5M price slash
news-global-econ.description: Helen Mirren and Taylor Hackford's 6.5-acre compound, built in 1911 for a silent film star, carries over a century of Old Hollywood legacy.

news-global-econ.description: Sen. John Fetterman, D-Pa., expresses concern over the Democratic Party's changing stance on Israel on 'Sunday Morning Futures.'

news-global-soc.title: Dems Blame Trump for Recalled Mexican Lettuce Causing Diarrhea
news-global-soc.description: A voluntary recall was issued for iceberg lettuce distributed to 27 states that has been linked to an “explosive diarrhea” parasite.

news-global-soc.title: Chicago: At Least 11 Shot Friday into Saturday Afternoon
news-global-soc.description:
At least 11 people were shot, two of them fatally, Friday into Saturday afternoon in Democrat-run Chicago, Illinois.
The post Chicago: At Least 11 Shot Friday into Saturday Afternoon appeared first on Breitbart.

news-global-soc.title: Trump administration touts more than $1B in UN budget cuts as reform push gains momentum
news-global-soc.description: The Trump administration says it secured over $1 billion in U.N. budget cuts in six months, including a $550 million peacekeeping budget reduction.

news-global-soc.title: Europe's fires reveal deadly mix of record heat, human error, arson and land mismanagement
news-global-soc.description: Record heat has fueled Europe's devastating wildfires, but experts say human error, suspected arson and land management have also played a major role in this summer's destruction.
